prompt: The device console tail turns to garbage whenever the serial bridge chunks unluckily. Lines like 'temp: 22°C ✓' land in the archive as 'temp: 22��C ...' — replacement soup — but only sometimes; re-running the same capture with different buffering produces clean text, so the bytes on the wire are fine. Two more problems from the same tailer: the last line of every capture is silently missing unless the device happened to end its output with a newline, and CRLF devices produce lines with an invisible trailing character that breaks our exact-match alert rules. Line numbers have to stay exact because the alert rules reference them, and a character the stream truncates at end-of-capture should still show up as a replacement character rather than disappearing. Repro: node --test test_line_stream.ts — it feeds the same bytes at every possible split point. Fix line_stream.ts; the tests are the contract and must not change.

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: Build/import of line_stream.ts exits 0. node --test exits 1 with 4 pass / 6 fail: chunk splits landing inside a multibyte character produce U+FFFD pairs instead of the character (the every-split-point sweep and the byte-at-a-time test fail), CRLF lines keep a trailing carriage return, the final unterminated line never comes back from flush, its line number is lost with it, and a character truncated at end of stream vanishes instead of flushing as a replacement.
